page_title: Shinji and Good Friends: Second Hanafuda Impact
text:
is a video game that was developed and published by GAINAX on 04 June 1999 for Microsoft Windows 95/98. It was sold for 7800 Yen (plus tax) when released.

Overview

A hanafuda game. The object of the game is to form combinations of cards called yaku. When a yaku is made, the player can choose to stop the round to earn the corresponding points, or continue to try to do better at the risk of losing. This game uses a specific deck of cards made up of 48 floral cards. The player faces his opponent in free mode, or in tournament mode. For beginners, there is a "help" mode for advice during the game. The game offers an "anger" gauge that increases as the player lose and the player character gets angry. When it is full, the player can "cheat" Pen Pen or the Spear of Longinus. This opus offers an atmosphere reminiscent of hot springs.

CPU: Hardware capable of running Windows95-J or Windows98-J (Pentium or later recommended)
RAM:16MB minimum (32MB recommended)
Display: Minimum 640x480 resolution, 256 (8-bit) color depth (800x600 resolution, 256 (8-bit) color depth or more recommended)
CD-ROM Drive: Dual-speed minimum (Quad-speed or faster recommended)
Audio: Requires PCM Soundboard
Hard Drive: 3MB for Minimal Install, 20MB for Standard Install, 80MB for Maximum Install http://web.archive.org/web/20020108192654/http://www.gainax.co.jp:80/soft/hanafuda/index-e.html

Trivia

The cover was illustrated by Takeshi Honda.
